items.project_definition: The RESTful interface implemented via FastAPI to expose authentication, course, and enrollment endpoints.
A logic gate that prevents the removal of a teaching asset if associated student registrations exist, triggering a 409 response.
Non-blocking execution pattern applied to all persistence layers and network calls using awaitable primitives.
The httpx utility used within test fixtures to simulate concurrent network requests to endpoints.
The SQLAlchemy 2.0 context manager for managing non-blocking database transactions via dependency injection.
A strategic build sequence prioritizing the identity layer to establish role-based boundaries before implementing domain features.
Deferred execution of notification services via FastAPI tasks to ensure registration responses are not delayed by external latency.
A framework primitive used to trigger asynchronous post-response operations, specifically for the greeting service.
A custom exception raised when an operation conflicts with a named domain constraint, resulting in a structured error response.
The implicit cross-origin resource sharing policy applied via middleware to regulate browser-based access to the interface.
The four foundational persistent storage mutation primitives used to manage courses and modules.
An exception triggered during system bootstrap if the required external platform key is missing from the environment.
A primary educational entity containing metadata, pricing, and a collection of sequenced learning modules.
The Pydantic v2 data transfer object used to validate the payload when establishing a new educational asset.
The filtered data structure returned to clients containing the identity and linked modules of an educational asset.
The Pydantic v2 schema defining the subset of editable fields for an existing educational asset.
The one-way transformation process implemented via passlib to secure user credentials before persistence.
The PostgreSQL relational store managing the persistent state of the system.
The deterministic response wrapping strategy used to ensure consistent output shapes across all endpoints.
A join entity representing the relationship between a student and a course, tracking completion percentage.
The Pydantic v2 request model used to initiate a student's registration for a specific educational asset.
The read-only data transfer object providing the current progress and timestamps of a student's registration.
The Pydantic v2 schema used to modify the progress percentage and completion status of a registration.
The relational constraint ensuring referential integrity between the student/instructor and the educational assets.
The standard format applied to all created_at and updated_at timestamps for temporal consistency.
The signed token standard used to maintain stateless session and role identity.
The interceptor layer used to encapsulate all outgoing responses into a standardized envelope.
A sequenced unit of content belonging to a parent educational asset.
The Pydantic v2 model used to define the title and sequence of a content unit during creation.
The data transfer object containing the identity and ordered position of a content unit.
The practice of assigning explicit identifiers to business logic constraints for traceability and error reporting.
The explicit instruction to defer service execution (specifically email) to a subsequent lifecycle phase.
The SQLAlchemy 2.0 object-relational mapping layer used to abstract all persistence without raw queries.
An exception raised when a user attempts to access or modify an asset they do not own.
The architectural style utilizing standard HTTP methods to manage educational assets and identity.
The specific logic requirement governing the deletion of assets based on the presence of active registrations.
The finalized output of an endpoint, always wrapped in the standard envelope with the appropriate HTTP status.
The official Resend library used to integrate transactional email services.
The language exclusively abstracted by the mapping layer to prevent direct query execution.
The toolkit providing the async mapping layer and session management for PostgreSQL.
The summarized high-level architectural overview of the system implementation.
The 80% code coverage threshold required for business logic verification.
The Pydantic v2 model containing the access and refresh credentials after successful authentication.
The identity entity capable of acting as either a student or an instructor within the platform.
The Pydantic v2 input schema used to verify credentials during the authentication flow.
The Pydantic v2 input schema for creating a new student identity.
The public identity profile returned after registration or login, excluding sensitive credentials.
An exception raised when a numeric input, such as progress percentage, falls outside the 0-100 range.
